issues.body: enumerate_review_items now calls provenance_admission_error(..., require_signature=False) so PLAN (secret-free) can still enumerate review items. But busy_packages_of_pulls (dispatch-claim.py:989) still consumes is_enumerable_provenance, which calls provenance_admission_error with the DEFAULT require_signature=True (dispatch-claim.py:1275-1279). busy_packages_of_pulls runs in the same keyless PLAN job (via filter_busy_area_items at dispatch.yml:589; the `plan` job at dispatch.yml:57 has contents:read only and no LEDGER_RECORD_HMAC_KEY). Keyless, worker-pr.ledger_record_signature_valid returns False (empty secret path), so provenance_admission_error returns 'provenance record signature is missing or invalid' for EVERY signed record, is_enumerable_provenance returns False, and each open worker PR falls to the else branch (dispatch-claim.py:1002-1003) reserving GLOBAL_PACKAGE. filter_busy_area_items (dispatch-claim.py:1042) then drops ALL impl items when any worker PR is open, and the human-parked / provably-inert-draft free carve-out (dispatch-claim.py:990-1010) is dead code keyless. This directly contradicts the busy_packages_of_pulls docstring's stated LINKAGE PARITY invariant (dispatch-claim.py:940-941: 'the source issue comes from the SAME validated provenance record enumerate_review_items admits (is_enumerable_provenance)') and reintroduces the frontier-collapse regression the docstring (963-967) documents as fixed. CLAIM's revalidate pass cannot restore items PLAN already dropped from repository['items'] (dispatch.yml:589). Net effect: new impl dispatch stalls whenever a worker PR is open (the normal steady state). Fail-closed for trust, but a genuine correctness/availability regression.
issues.fix_hint: Give busy_packages_of_pulls / filter_busy_area_items the same structure-only admission the enumerator uses on the keyless PLAN side. E.g. thread a require_signature flag through is_enumerable_provenance and call it with require_signature=False from busy_packages_of_pulls (dispatch-claim.py:989), matching enumerate_review_items — the busy partition is a PLAN-side conflict-avoidance mechanism, not a trust boundary, and CLAIM's revalidate_items_against_live_pulls re-checks with the key. Alternatively, if the busy partition must stay authenticated, the `plan` job would need the key (which breaks the 'secret-free target half' posture and is worse). Either way, add a self-test that runs filter_busy_area_items/busy_packages_of_pulls WITHOUT LEDGER_RECORD_HMAC_KEY over an open, signed worker-PR fixture and asserts its source-issue crate (not GLOBAL) is reserved and the parked-draft carve-out still fires — the existing keyless test at dispatch-claim.py:4529-4541 does not cover this path.
issues.title: PLAN busy-area partition breaks linkage parity: keyless is_enumerable_provenance reserves GLOBAL for every worker PR, halting impl dispatch

issues.body: _registry_record_equivalent (worker-pr.py:1350-1351) now returns False whenever the STORED record lacks a valid signature. For a pre-migration legacy unsigned provenance/verdict record, a trusted writer re-recording the same logical content now hits _registry_put_file's non-equivalence branch (worker-pr.py:1401-1403 for the master/legacy probe, or 1417-1418 for the ledger probe) and RAISES 'already exists with different content', rather than the idempotent-success (return False) it produced before. data/README.md and orchestration/provenance/README.md state legacy unsigned envelopes 'must be re-recorded by a trusted writer', but this code path refuses the overwrite (records are create-only), so re-recording would require deleting the stale file out-of-band first. This is fail-closed and only affects pre-migration records, but the documented re-record story does not actually work as written.
issues.fix_hint: Either document that migrating a legacy unsigned record requires deleting the old ledger/master file before the trusted re-write, or handle the specific case (stored logically-equal but unsigned) as an explicit upgrade-in-place rather than a hard conflict. At minimum reconcile the README wording with the actual create-only refusal behavior.
issues.title: Legacy unsigned record can no longer be idempotently re-recorded; _registry_put_file raises instead of the README's 're-recorded by a trusted writer'

verdict.summary: The HMAC-SHA256 ledger authentication is well-built in isolation (constant-time compare via hmac.compare_digest, canonical sort_keys/compact JSON payload, absent-key => hard stop on the write side, fail-closed reject on missing/tampered signatures, verdict envelope + backfill/groom/review-fix consumers all authenticated). But the PLAN-vs-CLAIM split is applied INCONSISTENTLY on the PLAN side. enumerate_review_items was correctly switched to require_signature=False, yet busy_packages_of_pulls (scripts/dispatch-claim.py:989) still calls is_enumerable_provenance, which defaults to require_signature=True. That function runs inside the `plan` job (dispatch.yml:453 "PLAN (unprivileged, secret-free target half)"), which deliberately does NOT receive LEDGER_RECORD_HMAC_KEY. Keyless, ledger_record_signature_valid returns False for EVERY (correctly-signed) ledger record, so is_enumerable_provenance returns False for every open worker PR -> each falls to the else branch (dispatch-claim.py:1002-1003) and reserves GLOBAL_PACKAGE. filter_busy_area_items then drops ALL impl plan items whenever any worker PR is open (GLOBAL blocks everything, line 1042), and the parked-inert-draft free carve-out (990-1010) never fires. This silently reintroduces the exact 'frontier collapse'/linkage-parity regression the docstring at 940-967 says was fixed over rounds 2-5, and CLAIM cannot recover items PLAN already dropped. It is fail-closed for trust (no misdispatch) but is a real correctness/availability regression that halts new impl dispatch in steady state, and it is untested: the new keyless self-test (dispatch-claim.py:4529-4541) pins is_enumerable_provenance=False keyless but never exercises busy_packages_of_pulls/filter_busy_area_items, so the divergence it introduces is invisible. This makes the change incorrect and incomplete.
